originalAlarm.description: `ops` could be null (null value originating from line 19) and is dereferenced in the call to `irq_domain_add()`.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE true)
     (= CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN true)
     (= CONFIG_OF_IRQ false))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_OF_IRQ () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Line 25: irq_domain_add(ops) where ops=NULL (set at L19, never reassigned when OF_IRQ=F). Inside irq_domain_add, `int irq = *ops` at L12 (ERROR) → null dereference. Known VBDB bug. TWL4030=T causes '#define IRQ_DOMAIN' (L4), so IRQ_DOMAIN=T is implied by TWL4030=T — PC stating both is redundant but not contradictory. Bug requires TWL4030=T (for twl_probe/main call chain) AND OF_IRQ=F (ops stays NULL). PC = TWL4030=T AND IRQ_DOMAIN=T AND OF_IRQ=F is effectively equivalent to TWL4030=T AND OF_IRQ=F. Classification: TRUE.
